findings: The watchdog treated normal signoff artifact churn as a health blocker because it fingerprinted the parallel signoff file itself and required exact fingerprint parity even while reporting a broken docs state.
Real cron-style overlap commonly produces exit 75 from runner/verifier due to the shared global lock; the watchdog was surfacing that as a runtime failure rather than a benign contention signal.
The watchdog used a shorter verifier timeout than the underlying verifier/runner path, making false timeout risk higher under cron conditions.

The reporting path could fail to produce a concise, usable broken-state status because stale signoff mismatch was added as a top-level blocker even when the real state was simply BROKEN and already explained by checker/agentic results.
repairsApplied: Raised VERIFY_TIMEOUT from 180s to 420s to match the real verifier/runner timeout budget more closely.
Removed docs_stack_parallel_signoff.json from artifact-state fingerprinting so normal signoff refreshes do not self-invalidate artifact-state tracking.
Added explicit lock-skip detection and treated exit 75 from verifier as acceptable when the verifier artifact is already green.
Treated runner exit 75 with the global-lock skip message as benign contention so stale/failing parallel signoff mismatch is not reported as a separate runtime blocker during a broken-state report.
Updated aggressive repair flow to stop cleanly on lock contention and to include verification runs in the pass evaluation.
Added regression tests for verifier lock handling and runner-lock broken-state reporting.
proof: Patched agents/docs_quality/docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py to stabilize signoff/runtime behavior under lock contention and artifact churn.
Patched agents/docs_quality/test_docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py with new runtime regression coverage.
blockers: The docs content itself is still BROKEN per agentic/editorial outputs; approved=true here only covers runtime/reporting robustness, not docs quality green status.

A full live end-to-end cron run was not allowed to finish within this subtask window because the underlying docs verifier/remediation path is long-running; validation is based on code-path inspection plus targeted unit coverage.
